summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #594 where the boot-md hook was a no-op because it fired on a read-only GatewayStart event and its result was silently discarded, by replacing it with the established workspace markdown pattern used by SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, etc.
summary.outcome: BOOT.md is now loaded per-session via PromptPersona and injected into the system prompt via append_boot_section(). The broken hook was deleted, all tests pass, and a PR was created and merged with a follow-up fix for a stale test caught by a bot reviewer.

repo: Workspace markdown files (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, MEMORY.md, now BOOT.md) follow a consistent pattern: loaded via load_*_for_agent() in config/loader.rs, stored in PromptPersona in chat/src/lib.rs, injected via append_*_section() in agents/src/prompt.rs
GatewayStart is a read-only event that fires once at server start, not per session — hooks returning ModifyPayload on it are silently discarded

The build_system_prompt_with_session_runtime() and build_system_prompt_minimal_runtime() functions have many call sites (7 in chat/lib.rs, ~20 in test code) that all needed updating when adding a new parameter
The crate exports public API through crates/config/src/lib.rs — new loader functions must be re-exported there
Greptile bot reviews PRs and caught a stale test (discover_hooks_registers_builtin_handlers in server.rs) that still asserted 'boot-md' existed after the hook was removed
The worktree has no remote configured — git pull from main is a no-op in ephemeral worktrees

code.finding: load_workspace_markdown() and agent_workspace_dir() are the base utilities; all workspace file loaders follow pattern: agent-specific path first, fall back to global with .or_else(load_boot_md)

boot_text injection point is after identity/soul but before workspace-specific details (agents_text, tools_text, memory_text). Uses shared append_truncated_text_block() with WORKSPACE_FILE_MAX_CHARS limit
discover_hooks_registers_builtin_handlers test enumerates expected hook names — must be updated when adding/removing builtin hooks
Deleted — the entire hook-based approach was architecturally wrong; boot content needs per-session injection not a server-start event handler

workflow: When adding a parameter to a widely-called function in Rust, expect ~20+ call sites including tests to update — an agent sub-task was used to batch-update test call sites
Running cargo check --workspace early catches broken imports before running full test suite
Clippy failures due to cmake/build issues on unrelated crates can be filtered by running clippy only on affected crates (-p flags)
cargo fmt must be run after edits — format check will fail even on minor whitespace issues

friction: Adding boot_text parameter to build_system_prompt_with_session_runtime required updating ~20 test call sites — tedious mechanical work that required a sub-agent
Multiple indentation levels in call sites (2-space, 4-space, 8-space) meant replace_all couldn't handle all cases in one pass
Clippy was run on --workspace but failed due to a cmake build issue in an unrelated crate, obscuring whether affected crates were clean — had to re-run with -p flags per crate
The greptile bot caught a stale test (discover_hooks_registers_builtin_handlers) that the implementation didn't catch — required a follow-up commit after PR creation
open_items: The stale gateway test fix was committed separately after PR creation — worth checking if there are other tests that enumerate builtin hooks that could go stale in future
docs/src/hooks.md was updated to remove boot-md and add BOOT.md to workspace context section — verify docs build correctly if there's a docs CI check
No clippy run on the full workspace succeeded (cmake issue) — the cmake-related build failure in an unrelated crate should be investigated separately
